findings.issue: Column names are interpolated directly into SQL without allowlist validation, enabling SQL injection via crafted filter column names
findings.suggestion: Validate column names against an explicit allowlist derived from the table schema before interpolation

findings.issue: ORDER BY clause accepts arbitrary expressions, allowing blind SQL injection through sort parameters
findings.suggestion: Restrict ORDER BY to allowlisted column names and directions (ASC/DESC) only

findings.issue: No pagination limit cap allows clients to request unbounded result sets, risking OOM on large tables
findings.suggestion: Enforce a maximum page size (e.g. 1000 rows) regardless of client request

findings.issue: Test does not cover SQL injection vectors in column names
findings.suggestion: Add test cases with column names containing SQL metacharacters (semicolons, quotes, comments)
